text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori — Shared Memory R Objects. Uses POSIX shared memory (Linux, macOS) Win32 file mappings (Windows) R’s ALTREP framework let multiple processes machine read physical memory pages. external dependencies. Requires R >= 4.3.0 (ALTLIST). API: share() → ALTREP shared object, map_shared() → open SHM name, shared_name() → extract identifier, is_shared() → test shared. shared_name() returns path-bearing identifier form <prefix>[i1,i2,...] sub-lists / elements (1-based) bare prefix roots; map_shared() accepts forms round-trips addressed object directly. ALTREP serialization hooks emit identifier wire form — transparent serialize() mirai. Nested VECSXP elements zero-copy: level inline self-describing MORL region inside parent SHM.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Run the full testthat suite devtools::test()  # Run a single test file devtools::test_active_file() testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-nested.R")

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Build package R CMD build .  # Check package R CMD check --no-manual mori_*.tar.gz # Generate documentation from roxygen2 comments devtools::document()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Zero-copy (SHM-backed): atomic vectors (type, attributes) data frame columns written directly SHM backed ALTREP consumers. Attributes serialize trailing region restore via SET_ATTRIB. Numeric Dataptr_or_null returns SHM pointer; Dataptr(writable=TRUE) triggers COW private copy. String Elt lazily creates CHARSXPs via Rf_mkCharLenCE; Dataptr_or_null returns NULL force per-element access. Nested lists (zero-copy): VECSXP/LISTSXP elements shared list written inline complete child MORL region data_offset. level wraps ALTLIST via lightweight mori_list_view (pointer + bounds + index). is_shared() returns TRUE sub-lists; shared_name() returns root SHM name plus 1-based bracketed index path (e.g. /mori_abc_1[2,3]); map_shared(shared_name(sub)) opens addressed sub-list / element directly. OS-level region name prefix [ recoverable via sub("\\[.*$", "", shared_name(x)). Pass-: R objects (environments, closures, language objects, NULL) returned unchanged. SHM created.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori write-host, read-many consumers, COW mutation. share() allocates new SHM region unique name; existing regions never mutated. host writes full region inside mori_create returning, consumers obtain name call returns — partial writes observable. Consumer mappings read-(PROT_READ / FILE_MAP_READ); mutations trigger COW private memory. locking implemented required; future change admitting -place mutation breaks model.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R exported functions single .Call wrappers. mori_create dispatches TYPEOF(x): Already mori-backed ALTREP (mori_owned_tag R_altrep_data1(x)) → return x unchanged. Idempotence required: re-sharing sub-list view must allocate fresh root region (break shared_name()). NILSXP → returned -. VECSXP/LISTSXP → mori_shm_create_list_call — ALTLIST per-element directory. Pairlists coerced VECSXP every level. Data frames go path. STRSXP → mori_shm_create_string_call — ALTSTRING offset table + packed strings. SHM-eligible atomics (REALSXP, INTSXP, LGLSXP, RAWSXP, CPLXSXP) → mori_shm_create_vector_call. Everything else → returned -. path returns via mori_make_result, chains host extptr ALTREP’s keeper chain (see Internal State Lifetime).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Writing SHM count--write two flavours: mori_serialize_count / mori_serialize_into (serialize.c, fallback serialized bytes) recursive mori_nested_size / mori_nested_write (altrep.c, whole MORL regions). count writer disagree even one byte, SHM region malformed consumers read garbage. change either side must mirrored .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Registered mori_altrep_init. Numeric types — mori_real, mori_integer, mori_logical, mori_raw, mori_complex — share mori_vec pattern SHM-backed data pointer. mori_string uses mori_str lazy per-element access via Elt Rf_mkCharLenCE. mori_list uses mori_list_view per-element directory lazy Elt cache uses R_NilValue “uncached” sentinel — fresh VECSXP naturally R_NilValue-filled, cache init zero work. NIL-valued elements sole singleton output mori_unwrap_element’s fallback path, aren’t cached; re-extraction returns R_NilValue identity preserved free.

what: COW invariant for new ALTREP methods
title: CLAUDE.md
text: method returning writable pointer (mutating) must check R_altrep_data2(x) first — non-null, vector materialized private copy SHM pointer longer authoritative. New Dataptr / coercion methods routinely forget . Dataptr_or_null returns materialized copy data2 set, SHM pointer otherwise.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: classes register Serialized_state Unserialize. Identifier wire form (single canonical shape). mori_format_chain single source truth used mori_shm_name (.Call) three Serialized_state methods. seeds stack-allocated path[MORI_MAX_PATH] rear leaf’s index (>= 0), walks keeper chain mori_owned_tag hops, prepending view->index (>= 0 ) buffer — root→leaf order falls path[rear..MORI_MAX_PATH) without second pass. Formats <prefix> (root) <prefix>[i1+1,...] (sub-object) caller-provided 1 KB stack buffer using shm->name_len (cached create/open time, per-call strlen). 1-based externalisation cues R’s [[]] semantics; parser converts back 0-based. Unserialize. mori_Unserialize delegates STRSXP × 1 state mori_shm_open_and_wrap; parse-success returns opened ALTREP, parse-failure falls expanded-state handling (COW-materialized ALTSTRING case). mori_shm_open_and_wrap runs mori_parse_identifier: prefix-routes magic-dispatch, path form routes mori_open_path_c (C-level core walks intermediate VECSXP directory via mori_make_view_extptr — bare keeper-chain extptr ALTLIST wrapper attribute restoration, since intermediates never observed — bounds-checking children parents, calls mori_unwrap_element leaf full ALTLIST + attrs constructed). Fallback full materialization : - COW-materialized vectors (data2 set) — returns materialized copy. - Nesting depth exceeds MORI_MAX_PATH (64) mori_format_chain.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL (mori.h) /mori_ (POSIX) / Local\\mori_ (Windows) single source truth shared shm.c’s name-format strings (MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL "%x_%x" POSIX, MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL "%lx_%x" Windows) parser’s literal memcmp. Change ; sides stay sync. Names variable-width hex (zero-padding); MORI_NAME_MAX = 30 bounds prefix either platform (Windows worst case: Local\\mori_<8hex>_<8hex> = 28 chars + NUL). mori_parse_identifier bounded single-pass: memchr length cap (MORI_IDENTIFIER_MAX) → literal memcmp MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL → two bounded hex-digit run scans separated _ (guarded p < eos, rejecting empty runs) → dispatch byte second run (\0 prefix-, [ path) → comma-separated 1-based indices per-token INT32_MAX overflow check, terminated ]. Indices stored 0-based. path-parse loop relies NUL-termination sentinel (every fail-branch’s predicate excludes \0), trailing p < eos checks aren’t needed . Sizing invariant: MORI_NAME_MAX + 2 + 11 × MORI_MAX_PATH < MORI_FORMAT_BUFLEN.
code: identifier ::= prefix [ "[" int1 ("," int1)* "]" ] prefix     ::= MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL hex+ "_" hex+ hex+       ::= [0-9a-f]+         # one or more, lowercase int1       ::= [1-9][0-9]*       # 1-based, no leading zeros

title: CLAUDE.md
text: map_shared unserialize share mori_parse_identifier shape validation differ failure mode: malformed input → NULL (wrong type/length, NA, missing malformed prefix, malformed bracketed path); well-formed identifier fails map → error (missing region, bad magic, truncated header, OOB path index, non-VECSXP intermediate). Preserve split mori_shm_open_and_wrap: collapsing either way breaks probe-vs-corruption distinction.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Magic identifier first 4 bytes: MORH (0x4D4F5248) atomic vector, MORL (0x4D4F524C) ALTLIST, MORS (0x4D4F5253) ALTSTRING. tables canonical spec; mori_serialize_into mori_nested_write implementations. MORH — atomic vector. 64-byte header, data starts byte 64 (64-byte aligned SIMD). Trailing serialized attributes () follow data. Attributes serialized pairlist; mori_restore_attrs unserializes applies via SET_ATTRIB consumer. MORL — ALTLIST. Header + element directory + per-element data regions. directory entry (32 bytes): data_offset(8) + data_size(8) + sexptype(4) + attrs_size(4) + length(8). sexptype semantics: 0 → serialized bytes (via serialize.c); STRSXP → offset table + packed strings data_offset; VECSXP → nested MORL region inlined data_offset size data_size (child’s header / directory / elements / attrs inline; parent’s attrs_size always 0 VECSXP children); → raw zero-copy data. non-VECSXP entries attrs_size > 0, attrs appended within data region data_offset + data_size - attrs_size. MORS — ALTSTRING. 24-byte header + offset table + packed string bytes + optional trailing attributes. offset table entry (16 bytes): str_offset(int64) + str_length(int32) + str_encoding(int32). str_offset relative start packed string area. str_length < 0 means NA_STRING. str_encoding cetype_t (0=native, 1=UTF-8, 2=Latin-1, 3=bytes). layout used inline STRSXP elements within ALTLIST regions (offset table starts element’s data_offset; element-level attrs use directory entry’s attrs_size field).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: SHM lifetime fully automatic via chained external pointer finalizers. Three interned symbols (C globals altrep.c): mori_shm_tag (Rf_install("mori_shm")) — tags SHM mapping extptr (host- consumer-side). Addr mori_shm *; finalizer: munmap. mori_host_tag (Rf_install("mori_host")) — tags host-unlink extptr created mori_make_result. Addr mori_shm * carrying name (POSIX) HANDLE (Windows); finalizer: shm_unlink / CloseHandle. Sits shm terminus keeper chain. mori_owned_tag (Rf_install("mori_owned")) — tags every malloc-backed extptr keeper chain: mori ALTREP’s data1 extptr (view, vec, str) bare keeper-chain extptrs created mori_make_view_extptr path-walk intermediates (ALTREP wrapper still hold mori_list_view *). Addr type ALTREP data1 recovered TYPEOF(x) ALTREP boundary: VECSXP → mori_list_view *, STRSXP → mori_str *, else mori_vec *. Bare keeper-chain hops always mori_list_view * (vec/str ever appear leaves). Standalone-vs-element carried struct’s index field (-1 standalone/root, >= 0 element/sub-list); redundantly recoverable data1.prot’s tag (shm_tag → standalone, owned_tag → element/sub-list). Common finalizer: mori_owned_finalizer frees addr. is_shared() pointer comparison mori_owned_tag. mori_shm_name() invokes mori_format_chain emit bare prefix roots / standalones path-bearing form (<prefix>[i1,i2,...], 1-based) sub-objects.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Host side (share): mori_shm_create allocates mmaps region; POSIX fd closed mmap (mapping stays valid). mori_make_result splits ownership — ALTREP wrapper gets mapping via mori_shm_tag extptr (finalizer → munmap); host extptr (mori_host_tag, finalizer → shm_unlink / CloseHandle) chained extptr’s prot. finalizers run ALTREP GC’d. R_RegisterCFinalizerEx(..., TRUE) covers session exit. Consumer side (map_shared / unserialize): mori_shm_open maps read-(size via fstat / VirtualQuery); fd closed mmap. shm extptr’s finalizer munmap — never unlink. lists opened prefix, shm extptr’s immediate child root view extptr (mori_list_view *, index=-1) becomes ALTREP data1; sub-lists accessed via Elt (index >= 0) chain parent view extptr way. path-form map_shared("/mori_x_y[,j,...]"), intermediate hops bare keeper-chain extptrs (mori_make_view_extptr, ALTLIST) — leaf’s ALTREP exists. chain shape identical mori_format_chain’s purposes either way. Element vectors hold mori_vec / mori_str extptr whose prot immediate parent view (shm extptr vec/str root). Element lifetime: leaves keep root SHM alive chain (leaf → parent view(s) → shm extptr → host extptr). Every R_MakeExternalPtr retains prot, chain survives even enclosing ALTLIST(s) GC’d. mori_format_chain chain walker: walks mori_owned_tag hops (always mori_list_view * — vec/str extptrs ever leaves), collecting view indices (skipping root view’s -1) hits shm extptr.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori.h — types (mori_shm, mori_buf, mori_vec index field, mori_list_view), declarations, MORI_ALIGN64, identifier grammar constants (MORI_NAME_MAX, MORI_MAX_PATH, MORI_IDENTIFIER_MAX, MORI_FORMAT_BUFLEN, MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL). shm.c — platform SHM create/open/close + finalizers sides. serialize.c — mori_serialize_count, mori_serialize_into, mori_unserialize_from, mori_sizeof_elt. Used fallback (non-zero-copy) ALTLIST entries directory sexptype == 0. altrep.c — ALTREP class definitions, mori_create dispatcher, recursive writers (mori_nested_size / mori_nested_write), consumer open+wrap (mori_shm_open_and_wrap), path open (mori_open_path_c), view constructors (mori_make_view_extptr bare keeper-chain extptrs, mori_make_list_view full ALTLIST + attrs), identity/name (mori_is_shared / mori_shm_name), identifier formatter / parser (mori_format_chain / mori_parse_identifier), serialization hooks, mori_altrep_init. init.c — R_init_mori, .Call registration table (4 entries: mori_create, mori_shm_open_and_wrap, mori_is_shared, mori_shm_name). .Call names match C function names; entry points take single SEXP.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori-package.R (package docs) share.R (four .Call wrappers — dispatch error handling C level).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: testthat edition 3. Entry point tests/testthat.R; tests tests/testthat/test-*.R grouped topic (file’s desc() strings authoritative). tests run unconditionally — nothing gated behind skip_on_cran environment variables.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Linux: SHM /dev/shm/ (tmpfs). Accessed via open() / unlink() directly avoid -lrt link dependency shm_open requires. MAP_POPULATE pre-faults pages. macOS: Kernel-backed SHM via shm_open / shm_unlink (libc, extra link flags). MAP_POPULATE -op (defined 0). Windows: Page-file-backed via CreateFileMappingA / MapViewOfFile. kernel32 always available. Host must keep mapping handle alive consumers opened (GC-chained host extptr handles ).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: roxygen2 markdown support; NAMESPACE auto-generated. MIT license. README.md generated README.Rmd — edit .Rmd re-knit, never edit README.md directly. CLAUDE.md .claude/ excluded package builds (via .Rbuildignore).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gao C (2026). mori: Shared Memory R Objects. R package version 0.1.0.9000, https://shikokuchuo.net/mori/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mori: Shared Memory for R Objects},   author = {Charlie Gao},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0.9000},   url = {https://shikokuchuo.net/mori/}, }

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared Memory R Objects → share() writes R object shared memory returns shared version → ALTREP serialization hooks — shared objects serialize compactly work transparently serialize() mirai() → ALTREP-backed lazy access — 100-column data frame one mmap; columns materialize first touch → OS-level shared memory (POSIX / Win32) — pure C, external dependencies; read-processes, preventing corruption shared data → Automatic cleanup — shared memory freed R object garbage collected

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: share() writes R object shared memory returns zero-copy ALTREP view. Shared objects serialize compactly via ALTREP serialization hooks, working transparently mirai R serialization path. Shared memory automatically freed object garbage collected.
code: library(mori)  # Share a vector — returns an ALTREP-backed object x <- share(rnorm(1e6)) mean(x) #> [1] -0.00126693  # Serialized form is ~100 bytes, not ~8 MB x |> serialize(NULL) |> length() #> [1] 124

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: shared_name() extracts SHM name shared object. map_shared() opens shared region name — useful accessing data another process without serialization:
code: x <- share(1:1e6)  # Extract the SHM name nm <- shared_name(x) nm #> [1] "/mori_a44d_1"  # Another process can map the same region by name y <- map_shared(nm) identical(x[], y[]) #> [1] TRUE

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared objects can sent local daemons — ALTREP serialization hooks ensure SHM name crosses wire, daemon maps physical memory. Elements shared list also serialize compactly — element travels reference position parent shared region, full data:
code: library(lobstr) library(mirai)  daemons(1)  x <- share(rnorm(1e6))  # Worker maps the same shared memory — 0 bytes copied m <- mirai(list(mean = mean(x), size = lobstr::obj_size(x)), x = x) m[] #> $mean #> [1] 8.510976e-06 #>  #> $size #> 840 B  daemons(0) daemons(3)  # Share a list — all 3 vectors in a single shared region x <- share(list(a = rnorm(1e6), b = rnorm(1e6), c = rnorm(1e6)))  # Each element is sent as (parent_name, index) — zero-copy on the worker mirai_map(x, \(v) lobstr::obj_size(v) |> format())[.flat] #>       a       b       c  #> "840 B" "840 B" "840 B"  daemons(0)

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Parallel computing multiplies memory. 8 workers need 200 MB dataset, 1.6 GB serialization, transfer, deserialization — 8 separate copies consuming RAM. mori eliminates . share() writes data shared memory . worker maps physical pages, receiving reference ~300 bytes instead full dataset — payload ~700,000 times smaller, translates significant saving memory usage well total runtime:
code: daemons(8)  # 200 MB data frame — 5 columns × 5M rows df <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(25e6), ncol = 5)) shared_df <- share(df)  boot_mean <- \(i, data) colMeans(data[sample(nrow(data), replace = TRUE), ])  # Without mori — each daemon deserializes a full copy mirai_map(1:8, boot_mean, .args = list(data = df))[] |> system.time() #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.671  13.785   8.459  # With mori — each daemon maps the same shared memory mirai_map(1:8, boot_mean, .args = list(data = shared_df))[] |> system.time() #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.003   0.002   4.875  daemons(0)

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: atomic vector types lists / data frames written directly shared memory, attributes preserved end--end. Pairlists coerced lists. share() returns ALTREP wrappers point shared pages — deserialization, per-process memory allocation. R objects (environments, closures, language objects) returned unchanged share() — shared memory region created. Diagram showing share() writing object OS-backed shared memory, memory-mapped processes using zero-copy ALTREP wrappers

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: data frame 10 columns lives single shared region. task touches 3 columns pays 3. Character strings accessed lazily per element.

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared memory managed R’s garbage collector. SHM region stays alive long shared object (element extracted ) referenced R. references remain, garbage collector frees shared memory automatically. Important: Always assign result share() variable. shared memory kept alive R object reference — result used temporarily (assigned), garbage collector may free shared memory consumer process mapped .

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared data mapped read-. Mutations always local — R’s copy--write mechanism ensures processes continue reading original shared data: Structural changes list data frame (adding, removing, reordering elements) produce regular R list. shared region unaffected. Modifying values within shared vector (e.g., X[1] <- 0) materializes just vector private copy. vectors shared region stay zero-copy. – Please note mori project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

what: mori: Shared Memory for R Objects — mori-package
title: mori: Shared Memory for R Objects — mori-package
text: Share R objects via shared memory share(), access processes map_shared(), using R's ALTREP framework zero-copy memory-mapped access. Shared objects serialize compactly via ALTREP serialization hooks. Shared memory automatically freed R object garbage collected.

title: Create a Shared Object — share
text: atomic vectors (including character vectors attributes names, dim, class, levels) lists data frames whose elements vectors, ALTREP-backed object reads directly shared memory. object (environments, closures, language objects, NULL), input returned unchanged shared memory region created.

title: Create a Shared Object — share
text: Attributes stored alongside data shared memory region restored consumer side. Character vectors use packed layout elements materialised lazily access. serialised (e.g. serialize() across mirai() call), shared object represented compactly SHM name (~30 bytes) rather contents. shared memory region managed automatically. stays alive long returned object (element extracted ) referenced R, freed garbage collector references remain. share() idempotent: calling object already backed shared memory returns input unchanged without allocating new region. Important: always assign result share() variable. shared memory kept alive R object reference — result used temporary (assigned), garbage collector may free shared memory consumer process mapped .

title: Create a Shared Object — share
text: Direct saveRDS() shared object writes SHM name, resulting file meaningful machine region still alive. portable storage transport across machines, materialise regular -memory copy first rlang::duplicate(), deep-duplicates object:

title: Extract Shared Memory Name — shared_name
text: Extract SHM region name shared object. name can passed map_shared() open region another process.

title: Extract Shared Memory Name — shared_name
text: x shared object returned share() map_shared().

title: Extract Shared Memory Name — shared_name
text: character string identifying shared object, empty string "" x shared object. sub-list element extracted shared list, string carries bracketed 1-based index path (e.g. "/mori_abc_1[2,3]"). map_shared() accepts forms; path-bearing form returns addressed sub-object directly. OS-level SHM region name prefix [ recoverable via sub("\\[.*$", "", shared_name(x)).

title: mori (development version)
text: shared_name() map_shared() now round-trip sub-lists extracted elements, just whole shared objects. Sub-object identifiers carry bracketed index path (e.g. /mori_abc_1[2,3]). Wire-format change (breaking): serialization format shared memory region naming scheme changed. share() Linux fails clean R error /dev/shm small (typical containers, limit can raised start) instead SIGBUS-ing mid-serialize (#14). map_shared() Linux longer prefaults whole region, restoring lazy first-touch access match macOS.

title: mori 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-21 Initial CRAN release.
